page: Vultr vhp-1c-1gb-amd is a High Performance family virtual server featuring a single shared AMD EPYC vCPU operating at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 1.0 GB of system memory and 25 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It is equipped with one complimentary public IPv4 address. The CPU features a cache hierarchy of 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache, supporting advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16) alongside hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

Benchmark data reveals a polarized performance profile. In single-core stress-ng testing, the server achieves a top-tier score of 3294.99, placing it in the top 10% of tested systems. However, because it is limited to a single shared vCPU, its multi-core stress-ng performance is identical and ranks in the bottom 25%. Memory performance is generally constrained, with a cached small-block read bandwidth of 151.27 GB/sec (bottom 10%) and uncached large-block read bandwidth of 36.16 GB/sec (bottom 25%), though large-block write bandwidth reaches an average tier of 29.11 GB/sec. Passmark metrics reflect these resource limitations, with a CPU score of 2738.52 and a database operations score of 965.62, both in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance represents a low-resource, entry-level tier designed for budget-conscious deployments where high density and minimal resource footprints are prioritized over sustained multi-core throughput. The inclusion of fast local NVMe SSD storage provides a responsive storage layer despite the shared CPU allocation. Given the 1.0 GB memory limit and poor database and Redis SET benchmark results (2.21 million operations per second), this server is not suited for heavy database workloads or high-traffic static web serving (which benched at 1.52 GB/sec). It is best suited for lightweight utility tasks, basic microservices, development sandboxes, or simple automation scripts that benefit from modern AMD EPYC instruction sets but do not require dedicated CPU threads or large memory pools.
description: The Vultr vhp-1c-1gb-amd is an entry-level virtual server from the High Performance family, configured with a single shared AMD EPYC vCPU running at 2.0 GHz, 1.0 GB of RAM, and 25 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and AES cryptography. While stress-ng single-core benchmarks place the CPU in the top 10% with a score of 3294.99, its shared-core nature and low memory capacity limit overall performance. Memory read bandwidth and Passmark database metrics fall into the bottom 10%, reflecting its resource-constrained profile. This server offers a highly cost-efficient option for lightweight, non-resource-intensive workloads. It is best suited for basic utility tasks, microservices, development environments, and simple automation scripts that do not require dedicated CPU resources or large memory allocations.
og_description: Vultr vhp-1c-1gb-amd is a High Performance virtual server featuring a shared AMD EPYC vCPU, 1.0 GB of RAM, and 25 GB of NVMe SSD storage, designed for entry-level workloads and budget-conscious utility tasks.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-1c-1gb-amd is a virtual server with a shared AMD EPYC CPU, 1.0 GB RAM, and 25 GB NVMe storage, optimized for basic utility workloads.
tagline: An entry-level shared AMD EPYC virtual server with local NVMe storage, optimized for lightweight utility tasks and budget-conscious development.
bullet_points: Single shared AMD EPYC vCPU at 2.0 GHz with AVX-512 support
1.0 GB system memory and 25 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Constrained memory bandwidth and database performance metrics
Best suited for lightweight microservices, utility scripts, and test environments
